seed_problem: Frontier-Eng JobShop / ALE-Bench: emit a machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence + no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).
mutation: input+theme: keep the classic job-shop makespan objective but generalize the input with per-job RELEASE (ready) times and a difficulty ladder that injects a heavy bottleneck resource and long steps, skinned as commissioning an array of telescopes that share scarce facility resources. The intractable 'optimum' in the anchor score is replaced by a checker-computed, always-feasible fully-serial makespan baseline B, so scoring is deterministic: ratio = min(1, 0.1*B/F).
title: Telescope Array Commissioning: Minimum-Makespan Instrument Schedule
strategies: serial baseline: run every step back-to-back on one global timeline in telescope order (feasible, makespan = B, ratio 0.1)
ECT list scheduling (append-only): repeatedly dispatch the ready step with earliest completion, appending after the resource's last busy time
gap-insertion dispatch: place each dispatched step in the earliest feasible idle gap of its resource, not just after the last op
multi-restart randomized list scheduling with gap insertion: perturb dispatch priorities across seeds and keep the least-makespan schedule
critical-path / shifting-bottleneck refinement on top of a good dispatch
